pr.title: Partial match ftCo_800C2FD8, and propagate new type info
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Partially decompiled ftCo_800C2FD8 in ftafterimage.c, improving the function from 1.28% to 98.99% matched per the decomp.dev bot report. The PR also propagated discovered sword-afterimage data types through fighter, Link, Mars/Marth, and sword item headers/sources, replacing raw UNK_T/padding fields with structured data where the diff gave evidence.
what_changed: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in ftCo_800C2FD8 with a near-complete C implementation for sword afterimage state updates.
Added a file comment to ftafterimage.c describing the unit as creating trailing afterimages for swords in motion.
Replaced placeholder include usage in ftafterimage.c with concrete dependencies: item.h, it_26B1.h, itsword.h, ft/inlines.h, lb_00B0.h, and baselib/gobj.h.
Modeled Fighter::x20B0 as an array of three structs containing two Vec3 fields, rather than a run of UNK_T fields from fp+20B0 through fp+20F4.
Added struct SwordAttrs in ftMars/types.h with pad_x0[0x14], int x14, float x18, and float x1C.
Replaced Link ftLk_DatAttrs fields x64 through x80 with struct SwordAttrs x64 and included ftMars/types.h to access that type.
Replaced MarsAttributes padding at x78 with struct SwordAttrs x78.
Changed it_80285314 from void to HSD_JObj* in both itsword.c and itsword.h, returning it_80272CC0(gobj, 5) instead of discarding it.
ftCo_800C2FD8 now handles two paths: item-backed afterimages when fp->x2101_bits_8 is set, and character data attribute-backed sword afterimages for Link/Young Link and Mars/Emblem otherwise.
ftCo_800C2FD8 now updates afterimage ring-buffer state through fp->x2101_bits_0to6, fills temp_r30 positions via lb_8000B1CC and jobj->mtx columns, and increments fp->x2100 up to 3.
smart_moves: Propagated type discoveries instead of keeping the partial match isolated: the fighter struct, Link data attributes, Mars attributes, and sword item API were all updated so the new ftCo_800C2FD8 implementation could use meaningful access paths.

Identified that the fighter afterimage storage at x20B0 is three repeated records of two Vec3 values, which cleanly explains the x20B0 through x20F4 region and supports indexed access by x2101_bits_0to6.
Unified Link and Mars/Marth sword attribute layouts through a shared SwordAttrs shape, making the function's common handling of da->x64 and da->x78 explicit.
Corrected it_80285314's return type to HSD_JObj*, matching its observed use as the source joint object for item-backed sword afterimages.
Kept matching-oriented compiler scaffolding such as PAD_STACK(0x8) and temporary variable names/layout visible in the C implementation, appropriate for a partial match.
Preserved evidence of game behavior and assertions: item-backed afterimages require an It_Kind_Sword item, otherwise the code reports "no afterimage item!" and asserts in ftafterimage.c.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function starts indexing through previously unknown object fields, update the relevant struct definitions immediately; the new types often improve both the current match and future readability.
Repeated unknown scalar fields in a large struct may actually be an array of small records. Here, eighteen 4-byte unknown fields became x20B0[3], each with two Vec3 values.

A helper previously typed void may need a real return type once a caller is decompiled. Check the helper body: it_80285314 simply returned the result of it_80272CC0(gobj, 5) once the caller needed an HSD_JObj*.
Cross-character attribute layouts can reveal reusable substructures. Link's ftLk_DatAttrs x64 and MarsAttributes x78 both used the same SwordAttrs shape for afterimage joint/float data.
Partial matches can still provide valuable project-wide type information; this PR's title explicitly frames the work as both a partial match and type propagation.

If the original code has undefined or questionable behavior, preserve and document it rather than silently inventing safety. The implementation includes a comment noting undefined behavior if certain fighter kinds do not have a sword.
naming_conventions: Unmatched/common fighter functions continue to use the ftCo_ address-based prefix, e.g. ftCo_800C2FD8 and ftCo_800C2600.
Newly identified data structures use descriptive names where evidence exists, e.g. SwordAttrs for sword-afterimage attributes.
Anonymous or not-yet-fully-understood struct members retain offset-based names such as x14, x18, x1C, x20B0, x20F8, x20FC, and x2100.
The fighter-local nested record was named struct Fighter_x20B0_t, tying the type name to its offset because its semantic name was not fully known.
Character attribute structs retain existing project conventions such as ftLk_DatAttrs and MarsAttributes.
Item functions retain address-based names such as it_802852B8 and it_80285314 until stronger semantic names are available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x8), suggesting stack-shape preservation mattered for the partial match.
Kept compiler-like temporaries such as temp_r30, var_r29, var_r5, and var_r0 in ftCo_800C2FD8, which is common when preserving decompilation structure for matching.
Modeled fp->x2101_bits_0to6 as a ring-buffer index over three Fighter_x20B0_t entries, resetting to 0 when the index reaches 2 and otherwise incrementing.
Set var_r29 to select a matrix column: item-backed sword afterimages use column 1 after it_802852B8, while fighter-joint afterimages use column 0.
Used lb_8000B1CC(jobj, NULL, &temp_r30->x0) followed by direct reads from jobj->mtx into temp_r30->xC, matching the function's position/orientation sampling pattern.

Represented the non-item path as a switch on fp->kind, with explicit cases for Link/CLINK and Mars/EMBLEM assigning SwordAttrs, and many other fighter kinds falling through a documented sword-related bug path.
Inlined assert/report strings in the implementation rather than keeping manually declared literal symbols, relying on normal string literal generation.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only issue comment was an automated decomp.dev report showing one unmatched-function improvement: main/melee/ft/ftafterimage ftCo_800C2FD8 improved from 1.28% to 98.99%, with +426 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_800C2FD8 or rename Fighter_x20B0_t/SwordAttrs fields.
Search for other uses of fp->x20B0, x20F8, x20FC, x2100, and x2101_bits_0to6 to refine afterimage state names.
Check whether SwordAttrs should live in a shared fighter or item header rather than ftMars/types.h, since Link imports ftMars/types.h only for this type.
Look for additional callers of it_80285314 to verify the HSD_JObj* return type across the item system.
Investigate the documented undefined behavior for fighter kinds listed in the switch without assigning var_r5.
